steps.title: Convert the first percentage
steps.content: Particle A's speed is $5{,}500\%$ of particle C's speed: $$a = \frac{5500}{100} \cdot c = 55c.$$

steps.title: Convert the second percentage
steps.content: Particle C's speed is $0.01\%$ of particle B's speed: $$c = \frac{0.01}{100} \cdot b = 0.0001\,b.$$ Solving for $b$ gives $$b = \frac{c}{0.0001} = 10{,}000\,c.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $10{,}055c$ matches the derived sum. Keep.
  • B: $5{,}600c$ would require $b \approx 5{,}545c$, far from $10{,}000c$. Eliminate.
  • C: $5{,}510c$ is far too small and likely comes from an incorrect percent conversion. Eliminate.
  • D: $1{,}550c$ is far below the actual sum. Eliminate.
A is correct.
